prompt:
Theorem title: Weighted L2 inequality

Ambient theorem statement:
Weighted \(L^2\) inequality on \(\mathbb{R}^d\).

Let \(d \ge 1\) and let \(f : \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathbb{C}\) be sufficiently regular and sufficiently decaying at infinity so that the integrations by parts below are justified, with \(f \in H^1(\mathbb{R}^d)\) and \(x f \in L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)\). Then

\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2 \le \frac{2}{d}\,\|x f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]

Target theorem name: n1_aristotle

Scratch file to rewrite: FormalIslands/Generated/n1_worker_20260405-212946_4eb3f9b0.lean

Target node:

{
  "id": "n1",
  "title": "Weighted \\(L^2\\) inequality on \\(\\mathbb{R}^d\\)",
  "informal_statement": "Under the stated regularity and decay assumptions, one has\n\\[\n\\|f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}^2 \\le \\frac{2}{d}\\,\\|x f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}\\,\\|\\nabla f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}.\n\\]",
  "informal_proof_text": "Start from\n\\[\n\\|f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}|f(x)|^2\\,dx=\\frac{1}{d}\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}(\\operatorname{div}x)|f(x)|^2\\,dx,\n\\]\nusing \\(\\operatorname{div}(x)=d\\). Integrating by parts and discarding the boundary term by the decay assumptions gives\n\\[\n\\|f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=-\\frac{1}{d}\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}x\\cdot \\nabla(|f(x)|^2)\\,dx.\n\\]\nNow\n\\[\n\\nabla(|f|^2)=\\nabla(f\\overline f)=(\\nabla f)\\overline f+f\\nabla\\overline f,\n\\]\nso\n\\[\nx\\cdot \\nabla(|f|^2)=2\\,\\operatorname{Re}(x\\cdot \\nabla f\\,\\overline f).\n\\]\nHence\n\\[\n\\|f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=-\\frac{2}{d}\\operatorname{Re}\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}x\\cdot \\nabla f(x)\\,\\overline{f(x)}\\,dx.\n\\]\nTaking absolute values and using \\(|\\operatorname{Re}z|\\le |z|\\),\n\\[\n\\|f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\\le \\frac{2}{d}\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}|x|\\,|\\nabla f(x)|\\,|f(x)|\\,dx.\n\\]\nApply Cauchy--Schwarz to \\(|x||f(x)|\\) and \\(|\\nabla f(x)|\\):\n\\[\n\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}|x|\\,|\\nabla f(x)|\\,|f(x)|\\,dx\n\\le\n\\left(\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|f(x)|^2\\,dx\\right)^{1/2}\n\\left(\\int_{\\mathbb{R}^d}|\\nabla f(x)|^2\\,dx\\right)^{1/2}\n=\\|xf\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}\\,\\|\\nabla f\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}.\n\\]\nCombining the two displays yields the claim.",
  "formalization_priority": 1,
  "formalization_rationale": "Only substantive node in the graph. It is a self-contained inequality with a short proof using divergence of the identity vector field, integration by parts, the product rule for |f|^2, and Cauchy-Schwarz, so it is the clearest local first formalization target."
}
